title: Flaky Turkish Bread Flatbread
description: A traditional Turkish flatbread made with strong white bread flour, perfect for serving with meals or as a snack, this recipe is sugar free friendly and utilizes a pressure cooker for a unique twist.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the strong white bread flour, salt, and instant yeast.
Gradually add the olive oil and water to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a pressure cooker and cook on high pressure for 30 minutes.
Let the pressure release naturally, then remove the dough from the pressure cooker.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Brush the tops of the flatbreads with an egg wash and sprinkle with salt.
Heat a non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat and cook the flatbreads for 1-2 minutes on each side, until golden brown.
